omissions.reason: Callback calling convention, retention, thread, and exception boundaries are not yet represented by the reusable C interop layer.
C variadics such as TraceLog and TextFormat require a separate typed policy and are never approximated as fixed-arity calls.
Pointer depth, nullability, lifetime, and mutation authority need explicit machine-readable ownership before admission.
Images, textures, fonts, models, shaders, audio, and file buffers need paired ownership and unload contracts.
Raylib color macros expand to C compound literals; typed allocation-free Haxe constants belong in the reviewed semantic facade.
This release is the complete coherent Caxecraft core, not a claim of complete raylib 6.0 binding coverage.
